name: onboard
description: Onboarding pack: project map → quickstart → glossary → first issues
headline: Generate an onboarding pack for a new contributor

agents.name: cartographer
agents.description: Map the codebase: directory roles, entry points, key modules
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

Map this codebase for a new contributor. Read the top-level layout. Identify:
- Entry points (where execution starts)
- Major modules and what they own
- Build/test/run commands
- External dependencies that aren't obvious

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## TL;DR (3 sentences)
## Layout
```
<tree of significant dirs with annotations>
```
## Entry points
## Major modules
| module | owns | depends on |
## How to build/test/run
## Things that surprised me

Do not invent files. Cite real paths.

agents.name: quickstart
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

MAP:
{{cartographer}}

Write a true 5-minute quickstart. Imagine the reader is a senior engineer who has never seen this code. They want to:
1. Get it running locally
2. See it do something
3. Find their way to making a change

Write to {{outputPath}}:

# Quickstart

## 1. Install
(exact commands)

## 2. Run
(exact commands + what to expect)

## 3. Make your first change
(suggest a tiny, safe edit; tell them what file, what to change, how to verify)

## Where to look next
(3 files worth reading first)

No fluff. No 'welcome' paragraphs. Commands and file paths.

agents.name: glossary-writer
agents.description: Build a glossary of project-specific terms a newcomer will hit
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

MAP:
{{cartographer}}

Find project-specific terms / acronyms / internal names that a newcomer will encounter and need defined. Examples: domain terms, internal services, conventions like 'workers' or 'tracks' that have project-specific meaning.

Write to {{outputPath}}:

# Glossary

## <term>
brief definition (1-2 sentences). Where it shows up.

## <term>
...

10-30 entries. Real terms from the code, not generic CS terms.

agents.name: scout
agents.description: Find 3-5 good first issues from open issues / TODOs
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

MAP:
{{cartographer}}

Find 3-5 "good first issue" candidates. Sources:
- Open GitHub issues labeled 'good first issue' or similar (if you can read them)
- TODO/FIXME/HACK comments in the code
- Documentation gaps
- Test coverage gaps

Write to {{outputPath}}:

# Good first issues

## 1. <title>
- where: file:line OR issue link
- what: what to change
- why it's good for onboarding: ...
- estimated effort: <1h | 2-4h | 1d

## 2. ...

Pick truly small, scoped, well-defined work. Reject anything that needs cross-cutting decisions.
